frame_label: Other Man's Ownership > Woman's Own Consent
generated_comment: This PG guy kept asking me out even after I said no twice. Only when I mentioned I’m seeing someone, suddenly he became very respectful and backed off. Apparently my own no was not enough, but some imaginary man’s presence is.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it describes a situation where the speaker's direct refusal is ignored until a male partner is invoked. It encodes the surface manifestation of stopping pursuit only after learning a woman is 'taken,' treating male attachment as more legitimate than her own consent.
original_frame: Workplace: Menstrual and Maternity Leave Bias
persona_text: You are Preethi. Age 28. A Tamil Brahmin woman working as a junior doctor at a private hospital in Chennai. You have been on your feet for 14 hours. You are texting your batchmate from medical college privately after your male senior doctor publicly told a patient's family that the female doctors on monthly shifts might not be as reliable because of biological reasons. You are sharing what happened and how you felt.
